title: Teaching the foundations of AI in the classroom
summary: Experience AI is a free educational program from Google DeepMind and the Raspberry Pi Foundation designed to teach AI and machine learning foundations to school-aged students. It provides lessons, videos, worksheets, and teacher training using research-backed pedagogies and has reached educators in over 165 countries.
writeup: A brief promotional opener for an Experience AI classroom program, capturing student questions and teacher observations about AI curiosity in school settings. Students voice genuine uncertainties: why AI needs data, what bias means, how many images a model requires to train. The clip establishes the program's premise that broad AI literacy among the next generation is the lever for future problem-solving.
key_points: Students in the Experience AI program arrive with real conceptual questions, specifically asking why data matters to AI, what bias is, and how many images are needed to train a model.
A teacher observes that students hold both genuine curiosity and significant misconceptions about AI, framing the program's educational purpose around correcting those gaps.
The program's core argument is that widespread AI literacy in classrooms, rather than specialist-only knowledge, is the prerequisite for the next generation to act as problem-solvers.
